task_description: Documentation-only deep interview for roadmap, spec workflow, scripts, and protocols for the app.
initial_idea: plan the roadmap and solidify a solid spec workflow and the most correct scripts and protocols to use in this app; documentation only, no code

rounds.question: What is the single primary outcome this app is supposed to deliver, and for whom?
rounds.answer_summary: User wants a smart auto music video editor: upload a song for analysis via hosted Ascension.js/API endpoint, upload or source video clips, chop/reorganize them by matching attributes—especially motion—so edits flow together and sync to music; user can work by song sections like verse/chorus. Also wants Codex 5.3 references removed from future docs/artifacts.

rounds.question: For the first serious version of this app, what should the roadmap/spec explicitly exclude, even if it sounds useful?
rounds.answer_summary: First pass should prove: audio loads/analyzes and useful data is visualized; user can adjust parameters; user can add videos, handle decoding/encoding/chopping, and preview results in real time or after a loading/render phase. No final export required yet, no model fine-tuning, no auth, no pro editor, no mobile app. Some visual representation of sections/chunks is required, but not necessarily a full pro timeline.

rounds.question: For this documentation/spec workflow, what am I allowed to decide without asking you again, and what must remain your call?
rounds.answer_summary: User grants full autonomy over documentation structure, roadmap phases, script/protocol recommendations, research approach, and removing Codex 5.3 references. Project should standardize on GPT-5.4-related references only. App may remain web-first for now, but documentation may consider desktop/Tauri if latency requirements point there. Core priority is very low/near-zero latency and avoiding overlapping analysis/playback work.

rounds.question: You said zero latency is critical. To turn that into a usable spec, what is the actual acceptable interaction budget for the first serious version, and what behavior would make you reject the architecture as too laggy?
rounds.answer_summary: User prefers an explicit per-section recompute/loading state with progress bar over trying to update fully in real time. Playback must remain smooth and musically aligned once ready. The unacceptable failure mode is playback lag that misses beat accents or causes clip switches to fall behind the music, making audio-driven editing feel wrong.
notes: Pressure pass complete by revisiting the latency/rendering assumption and converting it into an architectural acceptance rule.

codebase_context.product_hint: video/audio beat-synced editing studio with upload, analysis, timeline segmentation, shuffle/join/split/ramp controls

latest_refinement.motion_engine: global motion field + residual motion + continuity score
latest_refinement.quantization_policy: hybrid, but onsets/transients should not be blindly quantized

final_refinements.motion_engine: global motion field + residual motion + continuity score
final_refinements.quantization_policy: do not blindly quantize onsets/transients; use hybrid fit policies
final_refinements.ranking_priority: musical alignment first, motion continuity default visual mode
